key_insight: A transfer-learning plus hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss upgrade to BiSeNetV2 substantially improves seam segmentation robustness under strong reflections without adding model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable real-time perception for autonomous construction welding robots on shiny metallic surfaces, where missed seams can directly reduce weld quality and automation reliability.
method: Transfer-learn a BiSeNetV2 seam-segmentation backbone for welding scenes
Optimize with a hybrid Cross-Entropy + Lovász loss to stabilize learning and improve overlap quality
Compare against OHEM-based baseline and other architectures (DeepLabV3+, UNet, SegFormer) under reflective conditions
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Performance gains are reported for reflective construction-welding data, so generalization to other seam types, camera setups, or non-reflective industrial environments still needs validation.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
